programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: Delve into the inside story of the murder of a 74-year-old woman and the macabre lengths the killer went to in attempting to conceal her death. Annette Smiths lodger reports that she has been missing for several weeks.

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: The suspect admits to the murder of a 74-year-old woman. But a deeply disturbing tale that shocks experienced detectives has only just begun. The police continue to probe the suspect and the interview becomes more and more chilling.

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Some extra brunch as Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer host the food and chat show.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil and Kirstie catch up with cautious first time buyers Sundeep and Rupreet in Harrow and postcode picky Hilary and Rob in Hitchin, Hertfordshire. Phil had the tricky task of widening the search beyond Hitchin. Once the couple saw what they could get for their money further out, it wasnt long before one property made it all worthwhile. Theyve been in their new home for just over a year.

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: With planning permission granted, Keith and Marj get to work removing the pews, parquet floor and pulpit in the main hall, and make a discovery in the once-pigeon-infested Sunday school ceiling vents.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In South Wales, Daffyd plans a nutritious vegetarian-ish dinner party, but kitchen chaos ensues. Will his dud pud ruin his chances of winning the grand?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The weeks third visit is to Barbara and Elizabeths Holly Lodge Boutique BandB in Norfolk. Some doubts about grout get things off to a bad start, but the hosts dance into the good books with an afternoon soundtracked by an old-school Wurlitzer organ.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: In France, new plans keep Andrew and Richard on their toes. Best friends Janey and Sharon start a new adventure. And will the floor-fillers fall flat at Abi and Darrens disco?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Musician Gerry and his manager Kathy live in Warwickshire, but yearn for the sea. They cant wait to buy a holiday home on the coast but theyre out of tune as to where it should be. Kathy loves the wild coastline of Pembrokeshire in the west of Wales, while Gerrys inspired by the crystal waters of the Greek island of Zakynthos.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Catching up with house-hunters who bought their dream place in the sun. Hows Simon and Lawrences renovation project in Charente going?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: First to host in Salisbury is Natasha Conti, whos laying on a summer fete, complete with hilarious prizes, and has asked her guests to dress in country casuals.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Farm manager Sophie McConnell plans pig racing as part of the entertainment on her night as host in Salisbury. Her home grown cow Levi provides the main course.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Former army major Anthony Brown-Hovelt is serving a worldly mix of dishes on his night in Salisbury, including Uzbekistani food and porridge ice cream.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Cost conscious Chris Hiller plans an eclectic menu for his night in Salisbury that includes chicken a lorange and peppers stuffed with baked beans.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Part-time care worker Jane Arnold is the last to host in Salisbury, and plans an English menu sourced from the great Wiltshire outdoors.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sue and Pete cant wait to start their new life in the Paphos region of Cyprus with their puppy Billy. Jasmine Harman helps them find a detached house with a pool for their £250,000 budget.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: From two first time buyers looking to settle down to a family who have been scarred by a previous purchase, this search challenges Kirstie and Phil in ways they never anticipated. Twenty-seven-year-old Mike Richie and his girlfriend Katy Roberts have recently returned home after ten months globe-trotting and want to put down roots in Leeds. Ann-Marie McCullough and partner Lee Connolly want to leave Leeds.

programme.title: Batch from Scratch: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: The Sellers family mealtimes are chaos thanks to dad Rudis long working hours and daily post-commute supermarket trips. Will they ever get dinner on the table before 9pm?

programme.title: Jamie: What to Eat This Week
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver takes us on a delicious journey, as he reawakens the kitchen garden after winter. He celebrates rhubarb in a lip-smacking sauce for marinated pork belly, and picks some new season spinach for a silky goats cheese risotto.

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: Its the semi-final and the potters make mosaics and water fountains inspired by Romes Trevi Fountain. Whose final-worthy creations will make a splash?

programme.title: Japans Tsunami: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Featuring previously unseen footage, the programme gives accounts from eyewitnesses, amateur photographers and video enthusiasts who caught the events of Japans cataclysmic tsunami on camera. It also grapples with the aftermath: the loss, uncertainty and long-term trauma faced by individuals and towns struggling to come to terms with whether they can or should rebuild their shattered communities.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing
programme.desc: Horse trainer Claire believes in country air and hard work. Her partner Bambi, a city-loving model and content creator, likes the easy life. Can Bambi keep up with Claires schedule?

programme.title: Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing
programme.desc: Can an eco-warrior and a petrolhead beauty salon owner agree to disagree on the environment? And a country-loving Yorkshireman shocks a London-based youth worker to the core.

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Hellfire

programme.title: Trucking Heavy
programme.desc: In Wales, haulier Ian gears himself up for a major cross-country mega-move that requires a police escort. Meanwhile, in Hull, mega-mover Steve is in charge of shifting a 38-tonne screener.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Young partygoers get lairy and the cops are assaulted in downtown Keighley, another young speeder writes his car off in a spectacular fireball, and cops pursue a car obeying all the rules of the road except the one requiring it to stop for the police.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Dog handler Steve rides to the aid of the armed response team after reports of a man drawing a gun on a pizza delivery driver, Charley and Wayne takeaway a drink driver at the drive thru, and Warren begins the foot pursuit to end all foot pursuits.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Sergeant Mick and the Operational Support Unit carry out a raid on a house with suspected drug activity, and uncover a cannabis farm in the loft. There is motorcycle madness as Gav apprehends a biker risking his and other road users lives.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britains Speeders
programme.desc: In Gateshead a child is knocked off his bike - but the wait time for an ambulance is three hours. Late night, Martain spots a speedy driver. It turns out to be a takeaway emergency.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Its foot to the floor time as Interceptors Richie and Harrison join the pursuit of a Toyota that fails to stop. The cops are horrified to discover a 14-year-old at the wheel.

programme.title: Police: Night Shift 999
programme.desc: The night begins with police officers are called to a fight in a takeaway and other officers respond to a hit-and-run call. There are reports of bouncers being racially abused by a passerby. Police receive a report of a man with a machete.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: Scully and Mulder discover a mysterious serial killer when they find that their current investigation is similar to an unsolved case from 1903.

programme.title: Mud Mountain Haulers
programme.desc: Things go south when Craigs brother Brent borrows a logging machine, leading to a risky mountainside recovery. Veteran log hauler Dan Millar battles rough roads and torn up tyres to make it to the mill.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Over the last three days, Steve Grahame has hauled 130 tonnes of freight a massive 2000 km, and his trucks taken a beating. Under the bonnet the news isnt good.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: In Warwickshire Dave Crouch comes to the rescue a tipper truck gets stuck in deep mud on an isolated farm track. And a 26-ton truck breaks down beside the London Eye.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Sister Harriet spots Terry waiting in the queue and decides he needs urgent care. Hes fallen off a roof and is unable to move his arm from a position above his head, "which immediately rings alarm bells", declares Sister Harriet.

programme.desc: Dr Dev Shah attempts to shell out a giant lipoma from the arm of a retired nurse, Dr Natalia Spierings deals with a chunky keloid, and Dr Emma McMullen tackles a pair of cysts nicknamed Tweedledum and Tweedledee.

programme.desc: The cops are cracking down on car thieves. Their hunt for a stolen car ends in a dramatic foot chase. Two reckless high performance car thieves lead them on the longest pursuit in the history of the force.

programme.desc: Ben travels to a secret location in the Appalachian mountains of North Carolina, where forest dweller Tod Kershaw lives in a primitive skills community who have chosen not to live as members of modern society, but as opportunistic outsiders who scavenge other peoples waste.

programme.title: Nazi Megastructures
programme.desc: A look at the Nazi occupation and defence of Norway, a frontline from which Hitler could attack the Soviet Union and build an atomic bomb.

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica is incredulous when private investigator Charlie Garrett bids $400,000 for a mediocre painting at an exclusive New York auction house - until the artist is murdered.

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: A successful frozen food company pledges $750,000 to Jessicas favourite charity, the Literacy Foundation. Unfortunately, when she arrives to accept the donation, the money has vanished and the company accountant has committed suicide. Soon after, the corpse of one of the firms co-founders is found in the company freezer.

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: Hassan Salah, first secretary of the Surian legation in Los Angeles, wants to see his country hold on to traditional ways. The young King of Suria, however, seems quite open to Western influences and ideas. The ruthless Hassan sees a way to get rid of a dangerous opponent, legation chief of security Youseff Alafa, and blame it on the protesting Surian students who support the King.

programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet
programme.desc: A chance to revisit some of the best cases as our Yorkshire vets come to the aid of all creatures - great and small.

programme.title: Worlds Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Cameras focus on the island of Taiwan, and three extraordinary railways. The journey begins in the eastern port city of Hualien, from where it travels north along the rugged Pacific coast.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Research into why and how large projects were built, the financial and social costs of their failure, and the consequences for the environment. Experts present plans to make something new from the ruins.

programme.title: Nazi Megastructures: Americas War
programme.desc: A closer look at the secret preparation and torpedo technology that was behind Japans devastating surprise air attack on Americas Pacific Fleet in 1941.

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: Dina Zughyar and Mostafa Jabbour turned their back on the American Dream to start from scratch with a handful of rabbits and chickens on a small patch of barren land in the shadow of the Atlas Mountains. In their mud house in Morocco, the couple confess to Ben that it has been much harder than they thought.

programme.title: Royal Heirs and Spares: Rivalry and Resentment
programme.desc: Documentary delving into more than a century of royal rivalries, uncovering the stormiest relationships within the monarchy - those between the Heir and the Spare.

programme.title: The Day the Queen Died: Minute by Minute
programme.desc: Insiders reveal the secrets of what really happened on the momentous day the Queen passed away. We hear from those at the heart of the story as the shocking news broke.

programme.title: Wentworth Prison
programme.desc: When new inmate Tasha is subjected to `prison justice for pushing the panic button, Doreen shocks everyone by turning her back on Bea and crossing the floor to join Kazs crew. Doreen realises that Nash has been lying to her.

programme.title: 22 Kids and Counting
programme.desc: Were back with a brand-new series following the lives of Britains Biggest Family, the Radfords. Its been forever since a Radford wedding, but at long, long last, one is coming!

programme.title: 22 Kids and Counting
programme.sub-title: 22 Kids and A Wedding - Part Two

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson visits the village of Staithes in North Yorkshire and unearths its role in kickstarting the chemical industry in the north east of England.

programme.title: Canal Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia starts this walk in Bath, where the Kennet and Avon Canal provided a 19th century `canal superhighway between the countrys two most important ports, Bristol and London. However, only 40 years later the trade along the canal was usurped by rail travel, leaving the once great waterway neglected and derelict. Julias 20-mile walk tells the story of how the waterway was restored to its former glory.

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: Dial B for Britain: The Story of the Landline
programme.desc: Timeshift journeys back to the age of the landline. a distant era when phones werent pocket-sized wireless devices, but bulky objects wired into our homes and workplaces.

programme.title: Children of the Tsunami
programme.desc: On 11 March 2011, Japan was hit by the greatest tsunami in a thousand years. Through compelling testimony from 7-10 year-old survivors, this film reveals how the deadly wave and the Fukushima nuclear accident have changed childrens lives forever. The story unfolds at two key locations: a primary school where 74 children were killed by the tsunami; and a school close to the Fukushima nuclear plant, attended by children evacuated from the nuclear exclusion zone.

programme.title: Boys From the Blackstuff
programme.desc: After his wife leaves him, Yosser Hughes struggles to hold his family together, but with no work and the police and social services closing in, he is driven towards a final desperate act.

programme.title: Boys From the Blackstuff
programme.desc: After his operation, George Malone walks out of hospital in his pyjamas. Chrissie, Loggo and Georges sons return him, but he later leaves again. His wife knows that he wishes to die at home, so Chrissie takes George out in a wheelchair to the docks where he once worked.

programme.title: Light and Dark
programme.desc: Professor Jim Al-Khalili explores the 99 per cent of the cosmos that is hidden in the dark, invisible to our telescopes and beyond our comprehension.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson visits the village of Staithes in North Yorkshire and unearths its role in kickstarting the chemical industry in the north east of England.

programme.title: Canal Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia starts this walk in Bath, where the Kennet and Avon Canal provided a 19th century `canal superhighway between the countrys two most important ports, Bristol and London. However, only 40 years later the trade along the canal was usurped by rail travel, leaving the once great waterway neglected and derelict. Julias 20-mile walk tells the story of how the waterway was restored to its former glory.

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: Dial B for Britain: The Story of the Landline
programme.desc: Timeshift journeys back to the age of the landline. a distant era when phones werent pocket-sized wireless devices, but bulky objects wired into our homes and workplaces.

programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Crimewatch Live
programme.desc: Essex Police need urgent help with finding a suspect of a gruesome murder. Also on the programme, how a gang brazenly stealing high-value cars was finally caught.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Three sets of developers who have all appeared on the show before tackle a variety of projects in Shropshire, County Durham and Staffordshire. Will their experience shine through, or are they destined to repeat mistakes of the past?

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and the teams try to uncover profitable antiques in Sussex with experts Caroline Hawley and Tim Weeks. While the reds and blues compete to make the most money at auction, Charlie learns how science can help to prove the authenticity of ancient artefacts, using a process called thermoluminescence.

programme.desc: Bisexual mum-of-three Sophie has brought a third person into her marriage to husband Ricki. Now she is scared she will be pushed out. Can they find a way to make this throuple work?

programme.title: Amanda and Alans Spanish Job
programme.desc: Amanda and Alan brace themselves to tackle the kitchen. With a love for eating and entertaining, it is crucial to the project that they get this social space perfect as they aim to push the boat out with a typical Spanish design.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: The presenters take the prospective buyers around to different houses in the country. The desire for more room, cleaner air and less noise drives city dwellers to seek an escape to the country.

programme.title: The Repair Shop: On the Road
programme.desc: Dom and Rich restore a storm-worn memorial in Kirkcudbright before a Remembrance service, and Will learns why Lyndsey is at her wits end trying to clean her prized cast iron pan.

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp, with sidekick Sarah Greene, hosts the quiz show where knowledge equals speed. Anne-Marie, Stephen, Savannah, Jan and Brandon race in moving podiums across the studio to try to win £5,000.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: A twist on the classic quiz show formula, contestants search through the depths of their knowledge in an attempt to score as few points as possible.

programme.desc: Priya gives an ultimatum for the sake of her family, Ross tries to help with a situation, and Lauren tries to prove herself.

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Knockout week continues as the ten remaining professional chefs are split into two groups. The first five head to Boxhall in central London to meet Marcus and Monica for their first challenge.

programme.title: Harriet
programme.desc: From her escape from slavery through the dangerous missions she led to liberate hundreds of slaves through the Underground Railroad, the story of heroic abolitionist Harriet Tubman is told.

programme.desc: With two interlinked rooms to tackle, Amanda steers the ship in the bedroom whilst Alan takes charge in the adjoining lounge, but creative tensions arise when Amanda wants to get rid of Alans favourite curved arch which links the spaces together.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Theres no place quite like Argyll and Bute, or so says Denise Nurses buyer, who wants to return there from Kent. Taking a break from the house hunting, Denise hops on a ferry to the Isle of Bute to meet an artist.

programme.title: Dragons Den
programme.desc: The pitches are for an oat-powered snack range, a father-and-son duos DIY gadget, real-world hide-and-seek adventures, and a sun-shielding lip care range.

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommys recovery is back on track after he and John put his hand to the test. The pair are training again, and the search for Tommys next opponent begins. Eager to make the most of family time, Tommy splashes out on a £90,000 motor home for family trips with Molly and Bambi and takes his mum out for lunch and a long-overdue catch-up.

programme.title: The Catch-Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve comes face-to-face with the longest venomous snake in the world, the king cobra, whose bite could kill an elephant. Later, he meets two of Thailands killer cats for a deadly cat face-off and then dons his climbing gear to scale a building in Bangkok.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve is in Uganda, getting up close and personal with some of our closest relatives - the primates. He meets gorillas in the mountains, squabbling baboons on the savannah and even has a dawn encounter with chimps in the forest.

programme.title: Dragons Den
programme.desc: In this episode, the pitches are a nose-friendly home composter, a GP with a medical bag redesign, a DIY drinks entrepreneur, and a husband-and-wife team with a pre-loved kitchen business.

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: This week, the candidates head to Egypt to put on corporate away days for paying clients. One team embarks on a desert adventure but ends up having a bumpy ride, while the rest set off on a lagoon tour which is anything but relaxing. Food service proves problematic, and for one candidate, theres a one-way ticket home.

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: Together with the weeks newly-fired candidate, Angela Scanlon and her panel will dissect all the drama from that weeks task, providing fans with in-depth analysis and behind the scenes intel.

programme.title: Living Every Second: The Kris Hallenga Story
programme.desc: With a party in a Cornish cathedral featuring Dawn French, Fearne Cotton, a cardboard coffin, and hundreds of friends, Kris Hallengas FUN-eral is the opening of a documentary that tells the story of Britains most vivacious cancer campaigner.

programme.title: Kensukes Kingdom
programme.desc: Young Michael and his dog wash up on a remote island when a violent storm sweeps them overboard during a sailing trip. Believing hes all alone, Michael is surprised to discover a mysterious Japanese man whos lived on the island since World War II.

programme.title: Men of the Manosphere
programme.desc: James Blake joins three young men to explore why theyve turned to the so-called manosphere to achieve success, and how its influenced their real lives.

programme.title: EastEnders Investigates: The Manosphere
programme.desc: Documentary on misogyny.

programme.title: My Mates A Bad Date
programme.desc: Khalil never remembers his dates names, so he calls them all baby girl to skirt around it. Obsessed with looking good, he checks himself out in the reflection of a spoon every 20 minutes on dinner dates.

programme.title: RuPauls Drag Race UK vs the World
programme.desc: The queens give drag makeovers to Team GB Olympians Greg Rutherford, Shanaze Reade, Molly Thompson-Smith, Celia Quansah and Daniel Jervis. Pop superstar Anastacia guest-judges.

programme.title: RuPauls Drag Race UK vs the World
programme.desc: The queens give drag makeovers to Team GB Olympians Greg Rutherford, Shanaze Reade, Molly Thompson-Smith, Celia Quansah and Daniel Jervis. Pop superstar Anastacia guest-judges.

programme.title: Marcus Wareing Simply Provence
programme.desc: Marcus time in Saint-Remy is nearly at an end. During his trip, hes been a sous-chef, been run over by goats, searched for snails, steered a combine harvester, and met some passionate producers. Now, hes been invited to a local farm party.

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Bear Grylls - Wild Reckoning
programme.desc: Former best friends Rhys and Niall work to repair their broken friendship. They drifted apart after the death of Nialls older brother and have barely spoken for two years.

programme.title: The Repair Shop: On the Road
programme.desc: Will and Dan get a model of the worlds only rotating boat lift back up and running, and Dom collects a precious toy that saw its owner through pioneering pre-natal treatment.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: In Scotlands Central Lowlands, Sonali Shah helps a Canadian couple to make a rural escape and meets Californians who bought their dream home, sight unseen, and never looked back.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: With the dominant males gone, trouble looms as intruder lions close in on the Xudum prides young cubs. Leopard Lediba searches for her missing cub after a tense encounter, and Pobe the cheetah must defend her familys food from bigger cats.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn invites a panel of guests to share their opinions on the newsmaking stories of the day.

programme.title: !mpossible
programme.desc: Thirty players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz show where players face a question and pick from a board of six rankable answers. Some answers are good, but only one is unbeatable.

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Susan Calman hosts the quiz show where being in control is everything. Who will emerge as the Boss and walk away with the cash? Only the strongest players will survive the game.

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway heads to Llandudno but he is not there to hit the beach, he is off to explore the rugged beauty of Great Orme, a giant headland overlooking the Irish Sea. Along the way he visits St Tudnos church and copper mines that date back four thousand years.

programme.title: New Zealand: Earths Mythical Islands
programme.desc: New Zealand was one of the last land-masses to be found and settled by people. Lush and fertile, almost everything brought here flourishes, often with surprising consequences. Told through the experiences of its native species - in particular, a charismatic and peculiar giant, flightless parrot - this is the moving story of the changing fortunes of New Zealands wildlife, since humans first arrived.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and a team of experts examine curios and artefacts offered up by the public. This episode was filmed in the Valley Leisure Centre in Biddulph, Staffordshire.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin is joined by antique experts David Barby and Phillip Serrell at The Council House in Bristol. They find a gold watch with a mysterious history and a collection of medals that leaves the owner unsure whether she wants to sell. programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo love to video chat with Muffin and Socks so they can all draw pictures together. When Muffin wont give Socks a turn, the kids discover what happens when they hog.

programme.desc: Bingo is playing Bad Mood with Dad, standing on his feet as Dad stomps around the house, controlling everything Bingo does and causing chaos.

Lars and Xuli must work together to mount a rescue operation at the bottom of the sea.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster has to deal with being the only monster in a town full of cute baby animals. Based on the `Love Monster series of books by award-winning British author and illustrator Rachel Bright.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing is playing the talking lettuce game on Flops phone when he drops the phone and accidentally breaks it. Too panicked to tell Flop what he has done, Bing puts the phone in the bin but hes so upset that Flop knows something has happened.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say tractor, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure! We see all sorts of tractors - a blue tractor, a pulling tractor, a stuck-in-the-mud tractor and a farm tractor. Theres a big tractor and a chug-along tractor.

programme.title: Lu and the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: Barnaby is in the mood for hugs, but not all his friends share the same enthusiasm for them. Barnaby learns to respect his friends feelings and to ask for consent.

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos watch a man practising his tennis strokes using a machine to shoot the balls to him. Great BigHoo turns the dial and the balls come out faster and faster.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends meet some very cool, feathered friends and learn to fly them. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble is taking a trip on an aeroplane... but who is the pilot?

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: In Tummy Tales, a group of children watch a plane loop-the-loop in the air, whilst the Teletubbies have magical fun watching a shooting star loop-the-loop. Later, the Tubby Toast goes loop-the-loop too as it flies out of the Tubby Toaster.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome babies Tommy, Remi, Felicity, Otis, George, Zadie and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Inside everyones bags in this episode are ribbons which are explored through discovery, play, song and story. The popular baby song `Wind the bobbin up features, as does a story involving Baby Bear. Plus, the Action Song gets everyone moving about before some calm time with bubbles and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: I Spy, You Spy
programme.desc: Elsee plays I Spy whilst on a beach walk with Mohamoud.

programme.title: I Can Grow It
programme.desc: Adrian, Mia and Aaron go on a tree-mendous garden adventure to discover the magical powers of trees.

programme.title: Dylans Playtime Adventures
programme.desc: Dylan imagines hes hotel manager wants to help a guest enjoy their stay at his hotel, but he soon discovers that giving his guest a good nights sleep can be pretty exhausting!

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: In this soft lullaby, Moon Baby observes the galaxy from up on the moon before floating back home to his cosy nursery.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: When Wasabi injures his leg during a game of shadow jump, Ginger and the Peas are forced to wait on him hand and foot.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Dad demonstrates to Bluey and Bingo just how difficult it is to look after a pet.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are exploring in the attic when they find an old cassette tape, and decide to make a mixtape for Duggee to remind him of all the happy memories theyve made while earning their badges.

programme.title: The Game Catchers
programme.desc: The Game Catchers land on the Salad Planet and make friends with three ladybug kids named Bug, Bugsy, and little Buglet. They are about to play the game Hurdles Relay, but first they have to solve the mystery of Buglets missing polka dots.

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Timmy is enthralled by Mittens new music box which has a splendid ballerina on top. When Timmy and Yabba break her precious toy, and despite their best efforts to replace it, Mittens is inconsolable, Yabba persuades Timmy to play the ballerina himself.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Ted and his family head to the harbour and have fun trying to catch crabs. Will the crustacean critters be tempted by the bacon bait? Back home, Ted helps Dad clean Mums car, and then they make delicious spaghetti and meatballs for the whole family.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Hannahs family is from South Korea, and she will be making a Korean salmon marinade, which is a popular recipe used there. She starts by adding garlic puree, Korean chilli paste, soy sauce, honey, toasted sesame oil and sesame seeds.

programme.title: Ferne and Rorys Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: Amiah-Rae finds out all about some of the smallest underwater creatures - seahorses. Chantelle meets seahorse fans Joss and Gabriel and puts on her wetsuit for an underwater safari.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Therapy dog Toffee and his owner, Helen, are visiting a lady called Anita, who lives on her own. Their purpose is to keep her company and listen to all her amazing stories.

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.sub-title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie: Tenrec and Tarantula
programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet a tenrec and a tarantula.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Its Bring-a-Toy-to-School Day, so Evie takes the trolley shes just been given for her fifth birthday. Waffle hides in it and ends up in Evies class, where he must keep very still so he isnt seen.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Oona really wants to learn how to jump like the experts.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: Its been snowing, and Yukee and her garden friends use music and beat to build a big snow band.

programme.title: My Petsaurus
programme.desc: Chloe puts on her very own version of Crufts for her to show off Topsys best tricks to Mum. However, Topsy seems more interested in the treats than performing.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee and the squirrels are tidying up before `Bring your Grandparents to Squirrel Club Day. Duggee has his grandparents badge, so he knows what they will like. A nice cup of tea.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Yakka Dee! celebrates children communicating - because talking fills you up with whizz, pop, bang! In this episode, Dee encourages a friend to say worm and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see a long worm, a little worm and a squirmy worm. Look out for the speedy worm and the greedy worm, the jelly worm and the welly worm! Dee enjoys a glow worm and we see a little girl make a muddy worm from her finger.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Numberblock Six is preparing for her board game club when Fifty-Six passes by outside. Six is a little mixed up, thinking its her guest of honour, and pulls Fifty-Six into the room. But what Six doesnt realise is that Fifty-Six is not the guest she was waiting for. Fifty-Six is a superhero, who just happened to be passing by. Suddenly, Fifty-Four arrives, a little late. Hes the guest of honour Six has been waiting for, and hes so excited to start playing loads of lovely board games.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: OI, OR and UR are in a coin-rolling, cork-popping, wave-surfing mad dash to prove who is the fastest! This episode introduces the-oi-, -or- and -ur- digraphs and the words join, coin, cork, surf, turf, cord, corn and end.

programme.title: Something Special: Were All Friends

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky knocks on a door and is welcomed by Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po. The children in Tummy Tales knock on their neighbours doors to share their homemade flapjacks. The Teletubbies have more fun with the Tiddlytubbies.

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Patchwork Butterfly has a problem: he doesnt like his trunk. The Patchwork Pals must find a way to show him how pretty and useful a trunk like his is.

programme.title: Chuggington

programme.desc: DIY enthusiast DIY Dan does his best to demonstrate how to give a garden fence a makeover. Keith Fitt and Arthur Sleep rock out and play air guitar in the studio. The future looks rocky for some valuable antique vases when Ann Teak attempts to play the lute. Ser.

programme.desc: Series for young children that celebrates lots of different amazing machines. In this episode, motorcycle rider Grace shows off three amazing food machines.

programme.desc: Ross tells Rachel that he cannot be around her once Emily comes to New York. Meanwhile, Chandler and Monica try to have a romantic weekend.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica and Rachel meet a monster, and will Ross obey Emily and move away from his friends?

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Moves In

programme.sub-title: The One with All the Thanksgivings

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Warning notes on Ross lunch causes concern among his co-workers, and Phoebe takes a literature class.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Inappropriate Sister
programme.desc: After returning from another disappointing audition, Joey tries to write a play in which he can star. Meanwhile, Rachel gets a date with her neighbour Danny, but is perturbed to discover his extremely intimate relationship with his sexy sister.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With All the Resolutions

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Chandlers Work Laugh

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: At her grandmothers funeral, Phoebe is astounded to meet her biological father, Frank Sr, who she has been trying to find for years. Elsewhere, Chandler tells perfectionist Monica that she is useless at massage, and Joeys fondness for an expensive bag loses him an audition.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Everybody Finds Out
programme.desc: Chandler and Monicas relationship becomes less of a secret, and Ross must prove to his boss that hes sane in order to return to work.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Girl Who Hits Joey
programme.desc: Chandler becomes anxious when he thinks Monica wants more from their relationship, and Ross moves into his new apartment and fails to impress his new neighbours. Meanwhile, Joey is afraid to end his relationship with a diminutive woman who punches him hard every time she thinks something is fun.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.title: Overhaulin
programme.desc: Noemi has been counting the pennies to pay for repairs to her Chevrolet Impala. Luckily, money and superior workmanship is no object for the Overhaulin experts.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: Treasure Quest: Snake Island
programme.desc: According to a legend that dates back to the 16th century, a cache of Incan gold worth hundreds of millions of dollars is hidden off the coast of southeastern Brazil. Its known as the `Treasure of the Trinity, and its eluded all who have sought it for 500 years. But a modern-day team of explorers follows a radical new theory to uncover its whereabouts: a remote, treacherous island where thousands of deadly vipers roam. Cork Graham leads the expedition to Queimada Grande - known to locals as Snake Island - armed with advanced technology to unearth complex clues and decipher puzzles that have protected the treasure for centuries. This Discovery series takes viewers along on the epic journey to one of the most dangerous places on Earth.

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: Dad annoys everyone with his old ventriloquist dummy Lord Luck, while Jonnys recently dumped friend Ben comes for dinner in need of cheering up.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers
programme.desc: This week in the studio, Sketch covers a shoddy Egyptian-themed tattoo for Ste that was originally done in a downstairs toilet by a Hells Angel. Jay is given free rein in designing a cover for Jake, whos managed to upset both his girlfriend and his mother with his ill-advised ink. Brave Amy calls on Alice to help her regain her body confidence after having a mastectomy.

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Jo Brand demonstrates an impressive grasp of street slang, Katy Wix uses a long part of her body to steal something and David Baddiel takes a sledgehammer to a problem.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Alan Carr and Josh Widdicombe take on Joe Wilkinson and Sara Pascoe. Bill Bailey is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent, and Rachel Riley.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Mitchell Modell is the CEO of Modells Sporting Goods, Americas oldest family-owned company selling athletics footwear, clothing and sporting equipment. He goes undercover within his company here.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Kat Cole, the President of Cinnabon Incorporated, which serves sweet treats from over 900 bakeries in 40 countries around the world, goes undercover among her own workforce.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Swansea and Bridgend, an Italian menu is overshadowed by terrible table manners and tactless banter, plus theres a starter of chicken lettuce boats - and a final showdown at the table.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Cumbria, Sarka and Pauls massive Czech portions defeat almost all of their guests and theres a musical extravaganza, plus a menu that takes a tour of Europe.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: Noam and Elas suburban party pad suited their lifestyle when it was just the two of them, but with the arrival of a baby girl, they need a house that better suits their growing family. If they are to sell their current place for top dollar, it needs a full makeover - both inside and out.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Essex groom Gary plans an aquatic wedding 200 miles from home, but Lauren hopes for a home counties service. Will Garys underwater dream sink without a trace?

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: While Sofia dreams of a boho beach blessing, Craig is planning an Oktoberfest wedding featuring short leather trousers. Will Sofia call time on their nuptials?

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: A couple and their three daughters, having outgrown their home, look to move into a bigger space - but mum and dad have different priorities.

programme.title: Tomahawk
programme.desc: Set during the uprising of the Sioux nation in the late 19th century, when broken treaties and a gold rush saw their tribal territories invaded once again. The film centres on Jim Bridger, a scout at Fort Phil Kearney, from where the 7th Cavalry rides to try and hold the peace, but the brutal slaying of innocents by a sadistic lieutenant sees the Sioux in full cry and only Bridgers cunning can help save the soldiers.

programme.desc: When the four outlaws who murdered his wife escape from the hangmans noose, vigilante Jim Douglas goes on a relentless revenge mission to hunt them down and make them pay.

programme.title: Send Me No Flowers
programme.desc: George Kimball mistakenly believes he only has a few months to live, and arouses the suspicions of his wife Judy when he tries to pair her off with Bert Power as a potential future husband.

programme.title: Notting Hill
programme.desc: Anna Scott is the most famous actress in the world, and William Thacker a second hand bookshop owner in Notting Hill. William must then summon all his charm, ingenuity and patience if there is to be a happy ending.

programme.title: The Father
programme.desc: A man refuses all assistance from his daughter as he ages. As he tries to make sense of his changing circumstances, he begins to doubt his loved ones, his own mind and even the fabric of his reality.

programme.title: The Rhythm Section
programme.desc: Stephanie Patrick veers down a path of self-destruction after a tragic plane crash kills her family. When Stephanie discovers it wasnt an accident, she soon embarks on a bloody quest for revenge to punish those responsible.

The film centres on Jim Bridger, a scout at Fort Phil Kearney, from where the 7th Cavalry rides to try and hold the peace, but the brutal slaying of innocents by a sadistic lieutenant sees the Sioux in full cry and only Bridgers cunning can help save the soldiers.

programme.title: The Bravados
programme.desc: When the four outlaws who murdered his wife escape from the hangmans noose, vigilante Jim Douglas goes on a relentless revenge mission to hunt them down and make them pay.

programme.title: Gunmans Walk
programme.desc: A veteran of the Wild West fails to adapt to the changing life of the frontier. His determination to mould his sons Ed and Davy in his gun-toting image backfires when one of them turns into a murderous desperado.

programme.title: Send Me No Flowers
programme.desc: George Kimball mistakenly believes he only has a few months to live, and arouses the suspicions of his wife Judy when he tries to pair her off with Bert Power as a potential future husband.

programme.title: Notting Hill
programme.desc: Anna Scott is the most famous actress in the world, and William Thacker a second hand bookshop owner in Notting Hill. After colliding in the street they begin a relationship which is soon fraught with complications as the tabloid press hound Anna. William must then summon all his charm, ingenuity and patience if there is to be a happy ending.

programme.title: The Equalizer 2
programme.desc: Robert McCalls mysterious past cuts especially close to home when thugs kill Susan Plummer, his best friend and former colleague. Now out for revenge, McCall must take on a crew of highly trained assassins who will stop at nothing to destroy him.

programme.title: The Father
programme.desc: A man refuses all assistance from his daughter as he ages. As he tries to make sense of his changing circumstances, he begins to doubt his loved ones, his own mind and even the fabric of his reality.

programme.title: The Rhythm Section
programme.desc: Stephanie Patrick veers down a path of self-destruction after a tragic plane crash kills her family. When Stephanie discovers it wasnt an accident, she soon embarks on a bloody quest for revenge to punish those responsible.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: In the third series, the search for Britains best amateur baker is back as 12 passionate baking fans compete for the title. Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins return as presenters, as do judges Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood, who devise the three challenges faced by the competitors each week. They incorporate a signature bake, which tests the bakers personality and creative flair; a technical bake, which tests experience; and a showstopper bake, during which the bakers are able to showcase their depth of skill and talent. Berry and Hollywood inspect, prod and sample the baked goodies as they decide who gets to have their cake - and eat it.

programme.title: James Martins French Road Trip

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by Michelin-starred chef Gareth Ward. They enjoy some Welsh Mountain cider tasting, then James uses some to dish up some cider- infused apple fritters with cider custard. Gareth discovers a cattle farm which rears Welsh Wagyu cattle who are fed on local beer. Later they take time out and Gareth is challenged to a thrilling rally car ride, while James demonstrates his dog training prowess with his trusty hound Cooper.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: Jamess final stop is in Englands first National Park: The Peak District. He is joined by cooking legend Tessa Bramley, who serves him up a delicious beef fillet along with vegetables from her beautiful garden. James takes part in an unusual contest, hen racing, where he challenges a local lad and world champion to a race. After discovering some award-winning gin, James meets a local forager and samples some of his elderflower wine.

programme.title: Quest for the Lost Tribes
programme.desc: Filmmaker Simcha Jacobovic searches the globe for answers and investigates the lost tribes of Israel. From 12 mentioned in the Bible only Judah and Benjamin remain after they survived the Assyrian purge of nearly three millennia ago.

programme.title: Forbidden Knowledge: Forgotten Cities Lost in Time
The Sacred City
programme.desc: Canadian researcher Dan Gibson argues that Muslims are praying in the wrong direction using archaeology, satellite photography, and ancient manuscripts. He suggests there is evidence that Mecca was not the original Holy City of Islam.

programme.title: Idi Amin - Ugandas Brutal Despot
programme.desc: Idi Amin, born in Uganda in the 1920s, is believed to have converted to Islam.

programme.title: The Evil Dictators of WWII
programme.desc: Delving into the lives of three of the most notorious dictators of the 20th century: Benito Mussolini, Joseph Stalin, and Adolf Hitler. Archival footage and expert interviews reveal how they rose to power and shaped the course of World War II.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: In the run-up to September 1939, political and economic chaos in the aftermath of World War I allowed Hitler in Germany, Mussolini in Italy and ultra-nationalists in imperial Japan to seize absolute power.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Germany took just four weeks to conquer Poland, using a new form of warfare - Blitzkrieg. Six armoured tank units known as Panzer divisions, supported by the Luftwaffe, quickly broke through Polish defences.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: After the retreat from Dunkirk, Churchill rallied Britain in its darkest hour. Hitler was only just across the channel, but he reckoned without our supreme strategist, the might of the navy and the manoeuvrability of the air force.

programme.title: May 1940: Churchills Finest Hour
programme.desc: For the 80th anniversary of Britains darkest hour Dan Snow visits the House of Commons to learn about the speeches that turned the tide of WWII.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Explores Hitlers assault on the Soviet Union in the summer of 1941, Operation Barbarossa and his attack on the Caucasus in 1942.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: The attack on Pearl Harbour brought the sleeping giant, America, into the war, and saw the emergence of the aircraft carrier as the pre-eminent weapon of the seas.

programme.title: Barbarossa: The Lost Diaries
programme.desc: Lieutenant Sander undertakes an arduous journey to Leningrad, revealing the harsh German east campaign of Operation Barbarossa. He notes down his experiences in a diary that provides us a look at the attack on the Soviet Union.

programme.title: Barbarossa: The Lost Diaries
programme.desc: Lieutenant Sander undertakes an arduous journey to Leningrad, revealing the harsh German east campaign of Operation Barbarossa. He notes down his experiences in a diary that provides us a look at the attack on the Soviet Union.

programme.title: Destruction
programme.desc: Bringing the best in television.

programme.title: After Auschwitz
programme.desc: After Auschwitz is a Post-Holocaust documentary that follows six extraordinary women, capturing what it means to move from tragedy and trauma towards life.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Mussolini wanted to recreate the Roman Empire, but proved to be a poor military strategist and his incompetence contributed to Hitlers eventual defeat.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Explores Hitlers assault on the Soviet Union in the summer of 1941, Operation Barbarossa and his attack on the Caucasus in 1942.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: The attack on Pearl Harbour brought the sleeping giant, America, into the war, and saw the emergence of the aircraft carrier as the pre-eminent weapon of the seas.

programme.title: Barbarossa: The Lost Diaries
programme.desc: Lieutenant Sander undertakes an arduous journey to Leningrad, revealing the harsh German east campaign of Operation Barbarossa. He notes down his experiences in a diary that provides us a look at the attack on the Soviet Union.

programme.title: Barbarossa: The Lost Diaries
programme.desc: Lieutenant Sander undertakes an arduous journey to Leningrad, revealing the harsh German east campaign of Operation Barbarossa. He notes down his experiences in a diary that provides us a look at the attack on the Soviet Union.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts travels back to the Ages of Bronze and Iron to discover what kind of a place Britain was before the Romans invaded. With no written history, only archaeology can provide the clues. Alice uncovers a world that is complex, sophisticated and pretty strange. She examines the two Hebridean Bronze Age skeletons known as the Cladh Hallan Mummies.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Does Everyone Hate the British Empire?
programme.desc: Al and comic Kevin Kropinyeri reveal that Captain James Cook was not really a captain, explore the Aboriginal history of Australian cricket, and uncover the often brutal land grabs that impacted Indigenous Australians.

programme.title: Secret Societies: In the Shadows
programme.desc: Yales ultra-secret society is a pinnacle of the elite, and its members have become presidents, Supreme Court justices, and heads of industry. To be "tapped" is to be welcomed into a line of power brokers with the promise of life-long opportunities.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: The River Hunters hit the oldest town in Britain, searching the towns historic waterway for evidence of the Siege of Colchester.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: The river hunters final hunt takes the team back into Yorkshire to uncover relics at Knaresborough Castle from the brutal 14th Century clashes between the English and the Scottish.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts tells the story of Roman Britain by studying the history of Chester, Britains most Roman town.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In York, Professor Alice Roberts takes part in a Viking battle, meets the metal detectorists behind one of the UKs greatest archaeological finds, and gets her hands on Viking poo.

programme.title: Cursed Treasures
programme.desc: Archaeologists and scientists help to uncover the truth behind the deadly curses bestowed on the finders of some of the worlds most fascinating treasures.

programme.title: Cursed Treasures
programme.desc: Archaeologists and scientists help to uncover the truth behind the deadly curses bestowed on the finders of some of the worlds most fascinating treasures.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts goes in search of our elusive Stone Age ancestors. Along the way she visits the Channel island of Jersey where she meets a team of archaeologists hoping to shed new light on the much-maligned Neanderthals, and embarks on a kayak survey of the coastline looking for undiscovered sites hidden in the cliffs. At the Natural history museum Alice sees the dark side of our Ice Age ancestors lives.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice discovers things at Norwich Castle Museum which highlight facts about Tudor life, Anglo-Saxon childbirth rituals, and the Pompeii of the Bronze Age.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: The river hunters final hunt takes the team back into Yorkshire to uncover relics at Knaresborough Castle from the brutal 14th Century clashes between the English and the Scottish.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: Battle of Culloden: Rick and Beau return to tackle new rivers in a brand-new series. From Wales to Scotland, the pair investigate the cold and murky waters of historic sites. (1 of 3)

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice visits Canterbury for the story of Plantagenet England. Canterbury is steeped in history and the era of the Plantagenets - the strong house that held the English throne from 1154 until 1485 - saw the most dramatic episodes in the citys story.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores the story of Stewart Britain by visiting Stirling, the home of the Scottish royals who eventually took the English throne in 1603 and created Great Britain.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson sets off on his most curious walk yet, across the ancient and turbulent kingdom of Northumbria. In the seventh century, at the height of the Dark Ages, the rule of Northumbria stretched from Edinburgh to York.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson travels along the wonderful seascapes of the North Norfolk coast and through one of the least developed spots in the country.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.sub-title: Viking Camp And Iron Age Gold
programme.desc: A look at the camp from which Vikings invaded Britain, and groundbreaking evidence that the world-famous Avebury stone circle isnt just a sacred site but a place where people lived and worked. In Staffordshire, the oldest Iron Age gold in Britain is unearthed.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: A look at the biggest collection of Roman writing tablets in Britain, giving insight into what Roman London was really like. Off the coast of Kent, a dive into the English Channel reveals the biggest marine excavation since the Mary Rose, an 18th-century East India Company ship packed with silver.

programme.title: Cursed Treasures
programme.desc: From a hidden tomb in Turkey to a death bed in Norway, uncover the origins of the curses surrounding the Karun Treasure, Edvard Munchs The Sick Child paintings and more. (S1, ep 9)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy looks at a flat in Kilburn in London with one of the worst cases of damp she has ever seen, while Martin and Dion are in the Midlands looking round semis with potential.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat with huge potential in Londons Earlsfield, and terraced houses in Wombourne near Wolverhampton and Ynyswen near Treorchy in Wales are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin is impressed with the potential of two shops and three flats in Tonypandy in Wales while Dion looks round a two-bedroom maisonette in Dawley and Lucy meets a first time developer with ambitious plans for an end terrace in Londons Forest Gate.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A first-floor one-bedroom flat in Londons Stepney Green, a three-bed semi in Dartford in Kent and a one-bedroom flat on the 17th floor of a block in Salford are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 1930s bungalow in Dereham in Norfolk, a first-floor flat in Londons East Ham and a property in Maryport in Cumbria with a hidden room are all sold at auction. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear their plans for their investments.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion visit a semi in Ilkeston in Derby, a flat in Streatham Hill in London and a bungalow in Somersets Portishead.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion visit a fifth-floor flat in Greater Manchester, a three bed semi in Carlisle and three commercial buildings near Stanstead.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An old Derbyshire farmhouse in Wirksworth gets a stunning make over, a developer builds a pair of semis in Chatham in Kent while a mid-terraced house in Blackhall Colliery in County Durham is given a makeover ready for the rental market.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion meet father and daughter developers. An apartment in a former mill in Mow Cop on the Staffordshire /Cheshire borders, a three-bed semi in East Ham and a flat in Bournemouth are all given makeovers with the help of Dad.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A couple about to get married buy their first home in Newcastle, a new dad thinks developing a flat in Leyton in London will give him more time with his four-month-old son, and a former IT worker develops a property in Gainsborough in Lincolnshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat over two floors in Londons Camberwell, another flat in Telford in Shropshire and a three-bed semi in Alvaston in Derby are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion visit a grand five-bedroom period house in Folkestone in Kent, a two bedroom flat in Londons Leytonstone and a two bedroom mid terrace in Fenton in Stoke on Trent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A studio flat in a former 1960s office block which is grade two listed in Londons Elephant and Castle, a terraced house in Pontygwaith in South Wales and a flat in in Cradley Heath near Birmingham are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A house with no floor in Edgbaston in Birmingham, a detached house on several levels in Oldham and a former garage complex ripe for development in Forest Hill in London are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A converted 16th century chapel in Matlock in Derbyshire, a two bedroom flat in Whetstone in London and a water damaged house in Wishaw in Scotland are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two bed semi in Meir in Stoke on Trent is given a country house look, a flat in Londons Willesden Green also gets the wow factor and a semi in Dudley in the West Midlands loses its 1980s look to be brought into the 21st century.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion meet property developers who have all bought at auction and hope to make a profit on their investments. Today a landscape gardener tackles a terrace in Ilkeston in Derby, two friends take on a four storey house in Sittingbourne in Kent while another couple of friends first venture together is a studio flat in South Norwood in London.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Terraced houses in Gravesend in Kent and Shipley in Derbyshire are given a new lease of life while former church premises in Harrow Weald in Middlesex are given a new use for the community.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The trio look at a family home in Kent, a former bar in Llanelli in Wales and a terrace in Croydon, all sold at auction. Will any of the developers make a return on their investments?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A three-bedroom semi in Crewe, a top-floor flat in London and a terraced house in Oldham are sold under the hammer. All the owners are happy with the prices they paid, but will any of them be able to turn a profit at the end of the projects?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: While developers in Croydon in Surrey and Donnington in Shropshire wrestle with the question of whether to lose a bedroom each for a better placed kitchen or bathroom, two friends in Derby consider creating two more in their six-bedroom house.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two brothers buy a rundown cottage in Probus in Cornwall, a father buys a flat in Mitcham in Surrey as a long-term investment for his young daughter and a seasoned property developer buys a house in Tunstall in Stoke-on-Trent for her daughter.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two brothers buy a rundown cottage in Probus in Cornwall, a father buys a flat in Mitcham in Surrey as a long-term investment for his young daughter and a seasoned property developer buys a house in Tunstall in Stoke-on-Trent for her daughter.

programme.title: Broken Trust
programme.desc: A real estate agent gives in to his wifes wish that her mother moves into their home. But what begins as a compassionate gesture turns into a bitter war of wills.

programme.title: Til Death Do Us Part
programme.desc: Dr Mark Wangler and his wife, Kathy, appear to be living the American dream, then a cold premeditated execution tears their family apart.

programme.desc: Brett and Mona Wehde live a quiet life in sleepy rural Iowa raising their three children; when a new family arrives, relationships are made, but behind closed doors a wicked plot is hatched.

programme.title: American Monster
programme.desc: In 1997, 78-year-old Jean Schwarzkopf was murdered in Florida. The retirees husband owned a jewellery business, was she murdered for her diamonds?

programme.title: Disappeared
programme.desc: When Holly Cantrell never returns home after work, her three sons are distraught. It soon comes to light that Holly harboured a web of secrets that may have led to her disappearance.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here
programme.sub-title: He Looks Like the Killer on TV
programme.desc: Margie Bult thinks her second husband is too good to be true from the start, but when she sees a fugitive on TV named Paul Steven Mack who resembles him, she vows to uncover the truth no matter the risk to herself.

programme.title: Breaking Homicide
programme.desc: Twenty-three-year-old Codi Joyce ends up dead after a brawl at a house party in 2015, yet nobody has been charged for his death, and witnesses wont talk.

programme.title: Breaking Homicide
programme.desc: Former Rhode Island police Sgt Derrick Levasseur and forensic psychologist Kris Mohandie answer the pleas of desperate families and investigate murder cases that have officially gone cold.

programme.title: Murder Under the Friday Night Lights
programme.desc: Former cheerleader Valerie Zavala is invited to a party with high school football players and cheerleaders; she is found dead; investigators collect home video from the party to find clues to identify her killer.

programme.title: Murder Under the Friday Night Lights
programme.desc: A look at the shocking and tragic cases of dangerous obsessions, brutal violence and even murder surrounding local high school football teams, tarnishing the sport these communities hold sacred.

programme.title: High Speed Chase
programme.desc: Georgia police officers chase down two suspects with an assault rifle who are intent on shooting their way out of trouble. A terrifying life-or-death pursuit results in multiple casualties before the gunmen lead police into a final, fatal gunfight.

programme.desc: After the murder of 15-year-old Milli Penn, Grand Rapids detectives investigate the masked shooter, rumoured to be a teenager linked to a fight club. Then, another shooting strikes and detectives must solve the case before more lives are lost.

programme.title: Real Time Crime
programme.desc: Police and communities have a new weapon in the fight against crime. Across America, Real Time Crime Centers use surveillance cameras, tracking systems and gunshot detectors to solve crimes and bring criminals to justice. These are their stories.

programme.desc: A fathers desperate search for his pregnant daughter leads him to an abandoned cabin in rural Canada. When police come in to investigate, a crucial surprise witness helps expose unspeakable crimes.

programme.title: American Monster
programme.desc: Joseph Green Brown was on death row for 13 years before his convictions were overturned. Decades later, he would be charged for the murder of his wife.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here: Shadows of Death
programme.desc: In 1997, in quaint Anne Arundel County, Maryland, a detective inherits a stack of cold cases. As he begins to unravel each one, he uncovers a shocking and gruesome common thread.

programme.title: Murder Chose Me
programme.desc: A mysterious accident on Christmas Eve leaves Tony Holden with a fatal neck injury, but the case goes unsolved; when Rod Demery reopens the investigation, he discovers a conspiracy between unlikely accomplices.

programme.title: Murder Chose Me
programme.desc: A 911 dispatcher is found slumped behind the wheel of a wrecked sedan with a bullet through his torso; to catch the killer, Rod Demery must retrace the victims steps and untangle a web of accusation and innuendo spun by a shady cast of characters.

programme.title: Evil Online
programme.desc: When Nikki McPhatter doesnt show up for her usual work shift, her friends immediately worry and point investigators to her online relationships.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the worlds longest running primetime drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour. Its themes - love, passion, deception, greed and intrigue - are ones which everyone can relate to.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.desc: Aidan and Mij host at the Earlston House Hotel in Paignton, Devon. One guest gets a bum deal with the bog roll and, at breakfast, theres kitchen confusion and sausage snobbery.

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.desc: Running resus for an extraordinary shift are two of the most experienced staff members in the AandE department. Consultant Emer and senior sister Jen deal with 8-year-old Pharell, who fell eight feet onto concrete while riding his BMX, and 19-year-old Dalvin who was heading to the West End to meet friends when his motorbike hit a bollard. As the night wears on, the Saturday night drink and drug casualties begin to roll in.

programme.desc: An episode that focusses on mothers and daughters coming to terms with mortality sees Jackie admit her terminally ill mother Josephine with breathing difficulties. programme.desc: The Chipmunks and Chipettes try to reduce their carbon footprint when Jeanette signs them up for a Green Living competition.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: After Brittany and the girls release a video that goes viral, Alvin, Simon, and Theodore try to top it. Simon falls ill and asks Alvin and Theodore not to go see a new movie without him, but Alvin just cannot resist.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: The town is under attack when Simon creates a drone that can not only deliver pizzas but has the ability to learn. Alvin worries the munks are responsible for making Dave boring when they find out he used to be a rock star.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: The Exchange Student; Crystal Ball Birthday
programme.desc: When an exchange student comes to visit, he tells a scary story that makes the chipmunks think Dave is in danger. Annie and Amber offer to plan Brittanys birthday party, pitting her against Jeanette and Eleanor in the process.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: After a big fight with Miss Miller, Miss Croner hides out in the chipmunks treehouse to make Miss Miller worried; Alvin bets Simon that time travelling is possible and hires an actor to pose as one, but a real one shows up.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin believes a neighbour is responsible for a recent diamond heist. Alvin tries to get out of the annual family day picnic with Dave to go to a big soccer game.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: The Chipmunks take in a stray dog, but have to keep the puppy hidden from Dave, who wont allow pets.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When Alvin finally pushes Miss Smith too far, she quits. Unfortunately, her replacement is a nightmare. Now, Alvin has to do whatever it takes to get her back.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Theodore gets trapped in a floor vent when he drops a puzzle piece down in it and tries to get it.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When one of Simons inventions malfunctions, Jeanette and Alvin switch places and now each must live as the other.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: Lost in Space ... Camp; Super Hot Chocolate
programme.desc: Theodore must prove hes got the right stuff when the Chipmunks and Chipettes head off to space camp. Theodore burns his tongue on some super-hot chocolate and loses his sense of taste.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: While training for a TV baking competition with Eleanor, Theodore develops an allergy to an unknown ingredient. // After a lice outbreak that closes the school, Dave sets up a classroom in the house acting as their teacher.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin books the band at a birthday party only to discover theyve been booked for the petting zoo as pets; Theodore invents an imaginary friend.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Dave worries that the Chipmunks have become addicted to a video game. A grounded Alvin begs Theodore to buy him a limited edition skateboard.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: Baby Whisperer; Lets Make a Deal
programme.desc: Alvin dresses Theodore up like a baby in order to impress the girl of his dreams. While attempting to make a special present for Dave, Alvin creates a snowball effect of chaos.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Everyone thinks Simon has lost his mind after he fails a school test and moves into a tree for some alone time. Alvin worries about his bad report card and does everything he can to intercept the mail before Dave sees it.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Follow the adventures of Alvin and the Chipmunks.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: A single father experiences the trials and tribulations of raising a group of young chipmunks who are also famous rock stars touring the world with their best friends, the Chipettes.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin sneaks into Simons new virtual reality game and is transported to a world in need of a hero; the chipmunks discover Officer Dangus has a twin brother thats a bully and encourage Dangus to stand up to him.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin plays a trick on Theodores pancakes. Alvin wants to take over the drum kit.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When an accident forces Simon to wear his old glasses, he becomes the target of the school bully. So Simon builds a homemade robot bodyguard!

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When Dave comes down with a bad cold, Alvin quickly offers to take over Dad duty. But he quickly learns that parenting is a lot harder than he ever imagined.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin and Brittany learn parenting is not easy when they have to care for a robot baby for a school assignment. When Alvins football team struggles, the coach makes the team learn ballet to help with their conditioning.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Theodore gets roped into doing Alvins babysitting job with a spoiled four year old. Kevin agrees to be Alvins assistant to get closer to the music industry, but ends up running errands instead.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: Of Mice and Munks; Simon Saves the World
programme.desc: The Chipettes fall in love with a mouse thats doomed to be fed to a snake as part of a science class; Simons new invention threatens to destroy the world.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and The Chipmunks
programme.desc: Dave writes a hit love song from a poem he found; since Simon wrote the poem, he panics his secret will come out.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Brittanys style icon proclaims Jeanette is a fashion trendsetter; Theodore thinks that he is a real superhero.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: The Chipettes set out to prove to Alvin and the boys that being a superhero isnt only a guy thing. Miss Smith tells Alvin that if he doesnt pass the next test, hell be held back a year and separated from his brothers.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When the boys accidentally break Daves new flat screen TV. they decide to become fugitives on the lam. Jeanette and Eleanor act as Sherlock Holmes and Watson when Theodores teddy bear mysteriously disappears.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When Dave gets a parenting app to help supervise the Chipmunks, Alvin tries everything to escape its control.
